fields.description:
# Attested signing: pin verified_at_sha via the signed manifest channel

## Why

A signed verdict is only worth anything if it is reproducible and bound to specific content.
Today the verdict signs a manifest and stores git `head_sha` as AUDIT METADATA ONLY
(signing.py) — nothing cryptographically pins WHAT code was verified. Attested mode must
resolve->pin->record->sign the immutable SHA. (Claim-gate ref-resolution coherence is a
sibling story; this slice is the attested-signing pin only.)

## What

- Bind the SHA through the EXISTING MANIFEST CHANNEL, NOT a new signed-payload field.
  `signing._canonical_payload` signs exactly `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and
  `sign_manifest`/`compute_signature` already sign the full `manifest: list[str]`. Carry the
  pin as a MANIFEST STEP (`verified-at-sha:<sha>`): it enters the signed bytes (bound +
  reproducible) WITHOUT touching `_canonical_payload` or bumping `PAYLOAD_VERSION` — so NO
  invalidation of prior certified closures. The manifest-vs-payload signing decision is
  recorded in the architecture ADR.
- Record `verified_at_sha` as a queryable ticket field (convenience metadata; the trust anchor
  is the manifest step). Keep the existing `head_sha` audit field AS IS (additive).
- Resolution boundary: resolve the client `ref` (default `origin/main`) to an immutable SHA;
  an UNRESOLVABLE ref in attested mode fails closed (no sign).
- Atomicity MECHANISM (named, not just asserted): the combined manifest pin + `verified_at_sha`
  ticket write is serialized through the ticket's existing event-append / optimistic-concurrency
  boundary (compare-and-set on the current SHA), so concurrent retries / races between two
  verifications against different SHAs cannot interleave (no check-then-act TOCTOU). Set-once
  per SHA; re-verifying a DIFFERENT SHA overwrites (no stale/double pin).
- verified_at_sha lifecycle: on REOPEN/abandon the pin is retired (cleared/invalidated) so a
  stale SHA never outlives its closure; local-mode runs never set it.
- Bar signing in `local` source mode: verify + report, NO signature.
- Shape the manifest pin so a future move to DSSE + asymmetric + transparency log is an envelope
  swap, not a rewrite (KEEP HMAC now; limits documented in S6).

## Scope

- In: manifest-channel SHA binding (no payload-version bump), `verified_at_sha` field +
  lifecycle, resolution boundary + fail-closed, atomic/idempotent/overwrite pin with a named
  serialization mechanism, local-mode signing bar, in-toto envelope shape.
- Out: claim-gate ref-resolution coherence (sibling story); snapshot/cache (S1/S2); HMAC-trust
  docs (S6, dependency).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An attested PASS adds a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` MANIFEST STEP covered by the signature;
      verifying certifies the SHA. No `_canonical_payload` change, NO `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump
      (regression test: pre-existing signatures still verify). The decision is recorded in the ADR.
- [ ] `verified_at_sha` is a queryable ticket field; re-running against the same SHA reproduces
      a verdict + manifest over identical content; `head_sha` audit field preserved (additive).
- [ ] The pin + field write is serialized via the ticket's event-append/optimistic-concurrency
      (compare-and-set) — proven by a concurrency/retry test (two writers racing, and a re-verify
      against a different SHA overwrites with no stale/double pin); NOT verified by inspection.
- [ ] On reopen/abandon the `verified_at_sha` pin is retired (no stale SHA outlives the closure);
      a `local`-mode run (dirty allowed) never sets it and never emits a signature.
- [ ] An unresolvable ref in attested mode fails closed (no signature).
- [ ] The manifest pin is shaped as an in-toto-style statement so the HMAC envelope can be
      swapped for DSSE/asymmetric later without changing the pin's data shape (covered by a test/contract).
